While it maintains a healthy 29.3 months of working capital, the program expense ratio stands at 51.9%, indicating a moderate level of direct spending on its primary educational initiatives [1][2].

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="5">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, ensuring that donor acquisition costs do not erode the value of gifts.
With <cite id="8">29.3 months of working capital</cite> available, the institution is well-positioned to withstand economic fluctuations without interrupting its academic programs.
The college is <cite id="9">licensed by the Illinois Board of Higher Education</cite> to grant Master's degrees, validating its academic rigor.

strengths_deep_dive: Darul Qasim's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and specialized curriculum. founded in 2001, it has built a <cite id="10">25-year track record</cite> in the American Muslim educational landscape. Its governance is supported by an <cite id="11">independent board of 8 members</cite>, which provides oversight for its <cite id="2">$10.2 million in total assets</cite>. The institution's ability to generate <cite id="12">$835,237 in program service revenue</cite> alongside donations indicates a healthy, diversified financial model that includes tuition-based income.
rich_narrative.summary: Darul Qasim is an Illinois-based institution of higher learning dedicated to the preservation and dissemination of traditional Islamic knowledge. The organization operates a range of specialized academic tracks, including the <cite id="1">Iftā Program and Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr</cite>, which focus on advanced Islamic law and Quranic exegesis. In the 2023 fiscal year, the institution reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $3,290,091</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="3">three-year revenue growth rate of 23.4%</cite>. While primarily an educational body, Darul Qasim maintains a <cite id="4">verified zakat eligibility policy</cite>, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations by supporting students and educational infrastructure. The organization demonstrates exceptional fiscal prudence in its development efforts, maintaining a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>. However, its financial profile is characterized by a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 51.9%</cite>, which is lower than the <cite id="7">peer median of 89.6%</cite> for international education charities. Despite this, the institution maintains a very strong liquidity position with <cite id="8">29.3 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, ensuring long-term operational stability for its faculty and students.
